items.title: Start with top-down memoized recursion
items.avoid: Designing a bottom-up table before you've stated the recurrence.
items.prefer: Write the recurrence as a recursive function; add `@lru_cache`; verify; only then port to a table if you need memory control.
items.why: The recurrence is the hard part; top-down lets you focus on it without iteration-order distractions.
items.example:
```python
from functools import lru_cache
@lru_cache(maxsize=None)
def solve(state): ...
```

items.title: Define the state precisely
items.avoid: Vague state ('the rest of the problem') that doesn't fit in a tuple.
items.prefer: Pin down the minimal state: `(i, j)`, `(i, capacity)`, `(time, hidden_state)`.
items.why: If the state isn't a small tuple of integers/hashables, you can't index a table or key a cache.
items.example:
```python
dp[(i, w)] = best value using first i items with capacity w
```

items.title: Compress space when only the last row matters
items.avoid: Keeping the full m × n table when you only read `dp[i-1]`.
items.prefer: Two rolling rows (or a single in-place sweep).
items.why: Drops memory from O(m·n) to O(n) — often the difference between fits-in-RAM and OOM.
items.example:
```python
prev = [...]
for i in 1..m:
    curr = [...]
    # fill curr from prev
    prev = curr
```

items.title: Reconstruct the actual answer when you need it
items.avoid: Returning only the optimal cost without the choices that achieved it.
items.prefer: Either back-trace through the DP table or store a 'parent' choice per cell.
items.why: Many tasks need the answer itself, not just its value (edit script, knapsack item set, Viterbi path).
items.example:
```python
# Knapsack: after filling dp, walk back through cells comparing dp[i][c] to dp[i-1][c] to learn which items were taken.
```

items.title: Use NumPy / vectorization when the DP table is large
items.prefer: Rewrite each row update as a NumPy expression when possible.
items.why: Vectorized updates run 10–100× faster on large tables.
items.example:
```python
import numpy as np
curr = np.minimum(prev + 1, np.minimum(curr_left + 1, prev_diag + (a != b)))
```
